architecture.summary: SDIGLM is a multimodal large language model built on VisualGLM-6B that integrates a U-Net-based semantic segmentation module to generate defect segmentation maps as visual chain of thought (CoT). The system processes images and text jointly, combining segmentation outputs with language-based CoT (via prompt engineering) and multiround dialogue fine-tuning to perform structural damage identification and generate natural language descriptions of damage characteristics.

biblio.title: SDIGLM: leveraging large language models and multimodal chain of thought for structural damage identification

claims.dataset: structural damage identification benchmark (infrastructure types unspecified)
claims.statement: SDIGLM achieves an accuracy of 95.24% across various infrastructure types
SDIGLM surpasses general-purpose MLLMs in structural damage identification
The model effectively describes damage characteristics such as hole size, crack direction, and corrosion severity
Integration of U-Net-based semantic segmentation module generates defect segmentation maps as visual chain of thought
Multiround dialogue fine-tuning dataset enhances logical reasoning capabilities for structural damage analysis
